methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
Multiverse Computing is a Series B AI & Software company that has raised $215M+ to date and carries a current valuation exceeding $500M. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom), alongside 1 public comparable (PLTR), reflecting the frontier-tech AI sector's composition.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $215M total raised proxy, yielding $408M. Peer-Median analysis across the 5 private comparables produces a median valuation of $5.0B, though the peer range spans $500M to $32.0B, indicating substantial dispersion. The median peer valuation reflects both early-stage AI companies and more mature software entrants within the Series B cohort.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $347M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.7B. This range positions Multiverse above its stated current floor of $500M. The wide dispersion in peer valuations—driven partly by the nascency and heterogeneity of frontier-tech AI—creates material uncertainty. Key variables include revenue scale relative to peers, capital efficiency, and market timing within a volatile AI funding environment. The stage-progression method alone suggests conservative downside; peer medians suggest considerably higher valuations are defensible for companies at this stage.
